channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Boniface Mwangi Advocates Harsh Measures Against Corruption and Outlines Governance Reforms
item.description: Nairobi — Activist and presidential aspirant Boniface Mwangi has sparked strong debate after proposing that corrupt public officials be hanged in public if convicted. Speaking on Capital in the Mornin...

item.title: LHRC warns over children being held in serious criminal case at Bunda Court
item.description: The Legal and Human Rights Centre (LHRC) in Tanzania has expressed strong concern over two minors involved in a serious criminal case at Bunda District Court. According to LHRC, the two children, iden...

item.title: ANC Sedibeng outlines strategy to stabilise municipalities and improve service delivery
item.description: The ANC in the Sedibeng region has announced that stabilising municipalities and improving service delivery will be its main focus ahead of this year's local government elections. Regional Secretary J...

item.title: King Wanda and The Don exit Big Brother Mzansi after reaching top 10
item.description: King Wanda, a 31-year-old municipal employee from Delmas, and The Don have officially exited the Big Brother Mzansi Bazozwa house following the live eviction show on Sunday. King Wanda expressed pride...

item.title: Pakistan Concludes 5G Spectrum Auction: 480 MHz Sold for $507 Million to Zong, Jazz and Ufone
item.description: The Government of Pakistan successfully completed its long-awaited 5G spectrum auction in Islamabad on March 10, 2026. A total of 480 megahertz (MHz) of spectrum was sold for $507 million after three ...

item.title: Lulu-Briggs Appreciates Tinubu’s Support for State Police and Restored Peace in Rivers
item.description: Chief Dumu Lulu-Briggs, former Accord Party governorship candidate in Rivers State, don commend President Bola Ahmed Tinibu for supporting State Police in Nigeria. For one-on-one chat with journalists...

item.title: Defence Argues Abbotsford Double Homicide Suspect May Not Be Guilty of Murder
item.description: Closing arguments continued in the trial of three men accused of the 2022 double homicide of Abbotsford residents Arnold and Joanne De Jong. The defence lawyer for Abhijeet Singh argued that while his...

item.title: Victoria General Hospital suspends surgeries due to Halifax power outage
item.description: Victoria General Hospital in Halifax, Nova Scotia, has temporarily closed its operating rooms following a power outage that affected the facility. The outage occurred early Monday morning, forcing the...

item.title: Accountant-General Explains February Salary Delay for Staff in Five Federal Agencies Due to Budget Shortfalls
item.description: The Office of the Accountant-General of the Federation don explain why some federal workers for Federal Ministry of Steel Development and four other agencies never collect their February 2026 salary. ...

item.title: Uzbekistan’s Uzum reaches $2.3 billion valuation as fintech and e-commerce ecosystem grows
item.description: Uzbekistan-based fintech and e-commerce company Uzum has achieved a $2.3 billion valuation, a 53% increase from just seven months ago, following a $131.5 million investment led by Oman’s sovereign wea...

item.title: Man Charged Following Fatal Stabbing in Logan Car Park
item.description: A 39-year-old man has tragically died after being stabbed in Logan, Queensland. Police allege the man sustained a single stab wound to the chest at a residence in Logan Reserve before being driven to ...

item.title: Police Arrest Nine Suspects Linked to Phone Snatching and Stolen Devices in Nakuru
item.description: In Nakuru County, police have successfully apprehended nine suspects in a major operation targeting mobile phone theft and resale networks. The intelligence-led crackdown took place at Shoppers Paradi...

item.title: Young Israelis celebrate Purim underground despite missile threats
item.description: During the recent Purim celebrations in Tel Aviv, young Israelis staged underground dance parties in bomb shelters and reinforced garages, demonstrating resilience amid ongoing missile strikes. The fe...

item.title: Lacson accuses Marcoleta of obsession with defending China in privilege speech clash
item.description: Senate President Pro Tempore Panfilo 'Ping' Lacson delivered a strong privilege speech in the Senate, criticising Senator Rodante Marcoleta for what he described as ego-driven provocations and an obse...

item.title: KSE-100 recovers strongly after sharp drop, ends day with gains
item.description: On Tuesday, Pakistan's KSE-100 index staged a strong recovery, closing at 9,696.98 points, which is an increase of 6.62 percent from its previous close. The day started with a rapid rise of 9,303.75 p...

item.title: Sheikh Ahmad Gumi Says Government Knows Terrorists and Supports Dialogue for Peace
item.description: Controversial Islamic cleric, Sheikh Ahmad Gumi, don talk say Nigerian Government sabi every terrorist by name and know where dem dey. For interview wey e give DRTV, Gumi yan say e no dey go negotiate...

item.title: Government unveils first V-level qualifications to boost vocational pathways and job readiness
item.description: The UK government has announced the first set of V-level subjects, a new vocational qualification set to launch from 2027, aiming to better prepare young people for the future job market. Education, f...

item.title: Dak Prescott and Fiancée Cancel Wedding Over Prenup Disagreement
item.description: Dallas Cowboys quarterback Dak Prescott don call off him wedding just one week before the big day after him fiancée, Sarah Jane Ramos, refuse to sign prenup. News report say the matter come up after P...

item.title: Critique of increased defence spending amid low productivity
item.description: The article critiques calls for increased defence spending in Australia, arguing that such advocacy represents an 'anti-productivity' agenda. Bernard Keane highlights that sectors receiving the bulk o...

item.title: ICG Urges Dialogue via Friendly Nations to Ease Pakistan-Afghanistan Border Tensions and Militant Threats
item.description: According to the International Crisis Group (ICG) latest report titled 'Responding to the Militant Surge on the Afghan Border', violence in Pakistan's border areas, mainly driven by the Tehreek-e-Tali...
